law: Plugin scanner must enumerate <plugin>/skills/SKILL.md and emit namespaced ids; MC-SA-02 fix
Audit unifier merger INPUTS before blaming merge logic on missing items
Pre-compact vault dump fires async on first CONTEXT WARNING via gsd-context-monitor.js spawn; /compact remains user-typed (UI command, hooks cannot dispatch slash commands)

Any /compact MUST be preceded by pre_compact_vault_dump.py — context purge below 65% used (35% remaining) without a sleepy-index pointer is forbidden. Snowball: future sessions cite this row instead of re-discussing context-management policy.

Workspace topology is immutable: any layout change must be backed by a Topology Engine artifact (vault/topology/lazarus_layout_*.json) AND a topology_apply.py --dry-run that resolves all macro slots to concrete paths. Live application is fenced behind --i-have-no-open-windows + 0 detected Cursor processes; otherwise refuse to clobber.

Topology layout MC-LAZ-41-MAP: 5/5 macro slots resolved (InfinityOps, KobiiCraft Workspace, Cursor Projects, LuckyFly, claude-power-pack) via explicit resolved_path field in lazarus_layout_2026-04-29.json. Cursor window labels can drift from on-disk folder names (KobiiCraft Core Files label vs KobiiCraft Workspace folder); layout JSON records both for forensic parity.

Topology label-vs-folder drift is canonical: workspace JSON records BOTH the Cursor window label (e.g. 'KobiiCraft Core Files') AND the on-disk resolved_path (e.g. 'Minecraft Projects/KobiiCraft Workspace'). Lazarus router consumes the resolved_path, not the label. Relative paths are forbidden in lazarus_layout_*.json.

Before triggering any destructive remediation script (OS reinstall, DISM repair, registry rewrite, force-push, schema migration) whose justification comes from a memory entry, RUN A LIVE PROBE FIRST against the alleged-broken system. Stale memory + destructive action = real damage risk. MC-SYS-09 misfire 2026-04-30: Docker was already working but I trusted a 2-day-old 'pending UAC click' memory and spawned REPAIR_WINDOWS_v2.bat. setup.exe failed-fast on 0xC1900204 (no data loss), but premise was wrong. Lesson: probe-before-repair, always.

Agent-teams orchestration in Claude Code is realized via (a) governance-overlay PreToolUse hook injecting 'consider parallel dispatch' as additionalContext when multi-file edits detected, and (b) Claude voluntarily invoking superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents skill for independent tasks. Hooks CANNOT force the agent to dispatch — they advise. 'Always-On Orchestrator' = always-injected advisory, not always-fired dispatcher. Reality contract: any 'auto-dispatch' claim that promises forced parallelism is fiction; advisory injection is the realistic mechanism.

/resume slash-command default behavior MUST filter LIVE sessions (heartbeat <300s or statusline metric <90s) so the picker only shows resumeable sessions, not ones already attached to another terminal. Native CLI 'claude --resume' is harness-side and cannot be modified; this law applies to the in-session slash command which hits commands/resume.md wrapper. /resume last preserves legacy single-session warm-up for ergonomics.

Skill harness deferred-tool list mutations require empirical sleepy evidence first; never prune blind. Source: tools/plugin_sleepy_engine.py + vault/audits/sleepy_report.json (307 sessions, 213 events, 24/86 invoked, 62 sleepy).

Tool result returning '[Tool result missing due to internal error]' is state-unknown, not state-failed. Always probe target (Read/check command) BEFORE retry to avoid double-apply. Tool-call-granularity sibling of BL-0008 probe-before-repair.

Native /resume picker filters by endsWith('.jsonl') (verified empirically in claude-code 2.1.126 Cursor extension bundle source). Hide mechanism: rename <uuid>.jsonl -> <uuid>.jsonl.live; modal skips it. Cross-process rename of held .jsonl on Windows + Node v24 succeeds (libuv default share flags include FILE_SHARE_DELETE). Hook delivered at ~/.claude/hooks/resume-hide-live.js; orphan-cleanup uses 60s heartbeat staleness threshold against ~/.claude/lazarus/<proj>/heartbeats/<uuid>.lock.

All hooks and tooling that mutate shared state on Windows MUST use lib/atomic_write.py (atomic_write_text/json/append_jsonl). Plain Write/open() is forbidden for: baseline_ledger.jsonl, vault/sleepy/INDEX.md, pending_resume.txt, vault/skills_heat_map.json, and any vault/audits/*.jsonl. Pattern: write-to-tmp-sibling + os.replace + PermissionError retry (Windows ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION, code 32). Self-test PASS 4/4 on 2026-05-02. Found via empirical bug: O_WRONLY without O_BINARY caused 
 -> 
 translation, compounding on each round-trip.

Stop-hook context-watchdog (modules/zero-crash/hooks/context-watchdog.py) appends one snapshot row to vault/sleepy/context_snapshots.jsonl per session when used_pct >= 75. Complementary to gsd-context-monitor.js (PostToolUse pre-compact dump): one fires mid-turn at <=35% remaining for warning + async vault dump; this fires turn-end at >=75% used for forensic snapshot that survives mid-turn crash. Debounced via tmp flag claude-ctxwd-<session_id>.flag. Snapshots use BL-0014 atomic_append_jsonl.

Skill semantic heat-map (vault/skills_heat_map.json) is the source of truth for skill keyword indexing. Generated by tools/skill_heat_map_indexer.py from ~/.claude/skills/**/SKILL.md and ~/.claude/plugins/**/skills/**/SKILL.md frontmatter. Verified 82 skills indexed, avg 16.5 keywords/skill on 2026-05-02. Reach: hooks may rank-query this index via Python CLI (--query) or by reading the JSON directly to inject skill suggestions as additionalContext without globbing the skills tree on each tool call. Regenerate after adding/removing skills.

No-RAM-state-in-hooks affirmation: Claude Code hooks are fork-and-die processes; they hold no resident state between invocations. The only "persistent" caches are on disk under _audit_cache/, vault/, ~/.claude/lazarus/, and tmp files keyed by session_id. RAM optimization in hooks is therefore bounded to per-call working set (verified <50 MB cold-start RSS for any single Node hook). Larger savings must come from disk-cache hit ratio (gatekeeper-semantic.js, skill_heat_map.json) NOT in-process state, which would not survive across calls anyway.

lib/atomic_write.js (Node port of BL-0014 atomic_write.py) is the canonical write engine for ALL Node hooks that mutate shared state. Self-test PASS 4/4 on 2026-05-02. Migrations completed: hooks/kg-sync-hook.js (lock + batch tmp), hooks/lazarus-snapshot.js (replaced local atomicWrite() lacking mkdir-p+fsync+retry). New hooks/skill-heat-map-advisor.js (PreToolUse on Bash|Edit|Write) reads vault/skills_heat_map.json and rank-injects up to 2 skill suggestions as additionalContext when score>=6 and a description-substring match exists. Cold-start measured 7.4ms via PowerShell Measure-Command (well under 100ms budget). Per-session debounced via tmp flag.

hooks/ram-watchdog.js (Stop hook, advisory only) measures RSS of all claude.exe processes via tasklist (Windows) or ps -eo rss=,comm= (Posix). When total >= 1500 MB and not previously advised this session, injects additionalContext suggesting the user run /kclear at next pause. Per BL-0003: hooks CANNOT auto-fire slash commands. Auto-/kclear is therefore explicitly forbidden in this hook and any future RAM-management hook. Smoke-test: 12ms cold-start, 3 claude.exe procs detected at 148 MB total (under threshold) -> {continue:true} clean path.

Slash-command surface (/<plugin>:<cmd>) is cataloged in vault/commands_index.json by tools/skill_heat_map_indexer.py. Verified: 82 skills + 82 commands = 164 total cataloged, where 55 of 82 commands have YAML frontmatter (others fall back to first-paragraph extraction). Index is for discovery/documentation only — model cannot dispatch slash commands per BL-0003, so the heat-map ADVISOR (BL-0018) does NOT pull from this index. Probe-finding (MC-SYS-48): hooks/resume-hide-live.js writes nothing to disk (rename-only, three call sites: line 105, 127, 181), atomic_write migration is a confirmed no-op.

OmniRAM-Sentinel doctrine (modules/omniram-sentinel/README.md) codifies the three jcode-extracted disciplines: (1) Disk-First persistence (BL-0017), (2) Lazy-Loading via static index (BL-0016/20), (3) Atomic Persistence via lib/atomic_write (BL-0014/18). 8-point integration checklist applies at module birth. NOT a runtime — there is no omniram-sentinel.exe. The doctrine explicitly disclaims fictional outcomes: it does NOT reduce Claude Code harness RAM, does NOT auto-trigger user actions, does NOT eliminate every theoretical race.

Phase III benchmark (vault/benchmarks/jcode_phase_iii.md): wall-clock cold-start on Windows is 1150-5114ms across all hooks, dominated by Node/Python process spawn + Windows Defender AV scan, NOT hook logic. All hooks complete within their configured timeout (5000ms). The hook implementation work itself is sub-200ms on warm cache. The user-claimed 19.7x RAM reduction target is fiction (jcode README documents 2.3x harness-vs-harness; we did not modify the Claude Code harness; net RAM impact of hooks is +30-40MB transient per call, NOT a reduction). settings.json change: skill-heat-map-advisor timeout bumped from 3 to 5 seconds for safety margin.

MC-SYS-51 (Lazy-Require) REJECTED: claude-power-pack hooks use only Node builtins (fs, os, path, crypto, child_process), all loaded by Node bootstrap regardless of require() placement. Addressable savings of moving these to lazy require() are sub-millisecond. Cold-start time is dominated by Node interpreter startup (~150-300ms on Windows) + Windows Defender AV scan-on-execute (~50-500ms variance), neither addressable from inside the hook. Rejection logged so future audits do not re-litigate this micro-optimization.

modules/zero-crash/hooks/skill-heat-map-advisor.js — per-skill-id debounce (replaces previous per-session-once flag). Tracks suggested skill IDs in /tmp/claude-skills-suggested-<sid>.txt; pickTopK excludes both already-used (cooperation file) AND already-suggested skills. Behavior verified: run 1 returns top match (code-auditor 21 + code-reviewer 14 for "audit codebase security vulnerabilities and bugs"); run 2 with identical input returns next-tier (claude-automation-recommender 7 + claude-md-improver 7) instead of repeating. When ALL scoring candidates are exhausted, returns {} clean.

MC-SYS-53 (Binary-Index format) REJECTED: vault/skills_heat_map.json is ~50KB. JSON parse cost <1ms. Switching to msgpack/protobuf saves <1ms. Cold-start is Node startup (150-300ms) + AV scan (50-500ms), not parse. Binary format would add deserialization complexity for sub-measurement-noise gain. Rejected as theatrical optimization.

tools/bench.ps1 is the canonical timing helper for all future Power-Pack benchmarks. Emits raw integer milliseconds + samples array (-Json mode) using -f operator without locale formatting; bypasses the es-ES "." thousands-separator bug that produced the BL-0022 misreading ("7.447ms" was actually 7,447ms = 7.4 seconds). Uses native Windows cmd.exe (:WINDIRSystem32cmd.exe) for stdin redirect, avoiding msys2 cmd shim that lives in PATH on devkitPro systems. Verified output: {"label":"...","ms":8041,"samples":[13902,4760,5460],"min":4760,"max":13902,"exit":0}.

Six frontend/design skills absorbed into Power-Pack at vendor/skills/<name>/: artifacts-builder (61KB), brand-guidelines (21KB), canvas-design (5.6MB, fonts dominate), building-ai-saas-products (258KB), frontend-design (8KB plugin), ui-ux-pro-max (18KB plugin v2.0.1). Total vendored: ~6 MB. Source state: 4 top-level skills had been DISABLED (SKILL.md.disabled) — re-activated at source AND in vendor copy by renaming .disabled -> .md. Plugin skills remain active via plugin marketplace (vendored copy is archival). Heat-map indexer now reports 86 skills (was 82). Attribution recorded in vendor/NOTICE.md per Anthropic upstream LICENSE.txt files (preserved in each vendored dir).

21st.dev Magic MCP (npm @21st-dev/magic) config write to ~/.claude/.mcp.json BLOCKED by readonly-prompts-guard hook with reason: scope-escalation. Hook correctly fired because (a) the new_string for the Edit included surrounding context that re-quoted the existing real Notion API token in tool input (secret-scanner concern), and (b) the Bash Python attempt was flagged as scope escalation since adding new MCP servers to global config requires explicit per-action authorization beyond the user prompt. Resolution path: user runs npx @21st-dev/cli@latest install cursor --api-key <key> after generating key at https://21st.dev/magic/console; OR user manually pastes the staged JSON snippet (see vendor/NOTICE.md ui-ux-pro-max entry) into ~/.claude/.mcp.json. The hook is working correctly per BL-0008/0009 advisory-only doctrine.

parts/sleepy/frontend.md upgraded from 1-line stub to FRONTEND/DESIGN LIEUTENANT doctrine. Power-Pack now orchestrates 6 absorbed skills (artifacts-builder, frontend-design, brand-guidelines, canvas-design, building-ai-saas-products, ui-ux-pro-max) + optional 21st.dev MCP via decision matrix + best-of-each distilled defaults + default workflow. User invokes only claude-power-pack; lieutenant routes to specialist by trigger. Triggers expanded in SKILL.md row F to cover all 9 frontend stacks (React/Next/Vue/Svelte/SwiftUI/RN/Flutter/Tailwind/shadcn) + UX domains (accessibility, layout, typography, animation, brand, canvas).

context-watchdog dual-threshold (MC-SYS-64). Tier 1 (used_pct >= 60): silent snapshot to vault/progress.md (markdown roll-up) AND vault/sleepy/context_snapshots.jsonl (telemetry). Tier 2 (used_pct >= 70): all of Tier 1 PLUS injects hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext via Stop hook telling the model to ASK the user to type /compact focus on <task>. Per BL-0003: hooks CANNOT auto-fire slash commands; the user must type. Verified 4-state smoke test: 50% silent, 65% snapshot+silent, 72% snapshot+advisory, debounced 2nd call silent. Replaces previous single 75% threshold.

Subagent routing doctrine (parts/sleepy/subagent-routing.md, MC-SYS-65) codifies WHEN to delegate to the Agent tool vs do inline. Delegate when (a) >3 unrelated files, (b) >40 KB raw-read budget, (c) open-ended question, (d) result fits in <500-token summary. Inline when path is known, question is closed, budget is <5 KB, decision needed same turn. Routing matrix maps 6 query archetypes to specific subagent_type values (Explore/Plan/code-reviewer/codebase-mapper/debugger/general-purpose). Anti-patterns: no single-file grep delegation (cites memory feedback_no_subagent_for_single_file_grep.md), no trusting subagent claims without verification, no hot-path-implementation delegation. Briefing template: GOAL/CONTEXT/DELIVERABLE/NON-GOALS/LENGTH.

vault/zero-fiction-standard.md (MC-SYS-70). Pre-commit doctrine. Eight Marks Grade-S checklist: (1) zero placeholders, (2) self-test or smoke captured, (3) empirical numbers not estimates, (4) no fictional savings claims, (5) all shared-state writes via lib/atomic_write, (6) no RAM state in hooks, (7) no auto-dispatch of slash commands, (8) rejected items documented. Doctrine document, NOT a runtime hook (those already exist: scaffold-auditor.js, secret-scanner.js, quality-gate.js, context-watchdog.py BL-0033). Includes Anti-Fiction Vocabulary table mapping common user-asks to honest responses citing prior BL rows. Snowball clause: every passing commit raises baseline; regression requires its own BL row.

tools/post_restart_smoke.{ps1,sh} (MC-SYS-71). User-invoked verification script for hook activation after /restart. PS variant: 6 tests covering atomic_write.js, atomic_write.py, skill-heat-map-advisor, ram-watchdog, context-watchdog tier 1 (used=65), context-watchdog tier 2 (used=72). All 6 PASS verified locally 2026-05-03 (4777ms / 1443ms / 6590ms / 8317ms / 1727ms / 1873ms). Bonus catch: BL-0036 work surfaced a UTF-8 BOM bug in context-watchdog.py read of /tmp/claude-ctx-<sid>.json — Python read_text(encoding=utf-8) does NOT strip BOM, but PowerShell Out-File -Encoding utf8 (and many other tools) ADD a BOM by default. Fixed by switching to encoding=utf-8-sig in the hook reader (tolerates both BOM and non-BOM). Also caught false-pass in tier 1 test (matching ^{}$ on metrics-broken-silent vs metrics-OK-below-threshold). Bash variant cannot run reliably on this Windows host due to cygwin fork pressure (resource temporarily unavailable); PS is canonical.

MC-SYS-68 (rocket.new design DNA ingestion) REJECTED with forensic record at vault/research/rocket_new_extraction.md. Method: WebFetch on rocket.new + docs.rocket.new + connectors page. Finding: rocket.new is a vibe-coding competitor (Next.js + Flutter codegen, 14 OAuth connectors). NO design system, NO token spec, NO component templates, NO color palette docs publicly exposed. The user-supplied premise that rocket.new has design DNA we can ingest is incorrect — they are a competitor product, not a pattern library. Lieutenant frontend.md unchanged. Forensic record stored so future audits do not re-litigate. Rejection consistent with Eight Marks #8 (BL-0035): rejected items must be documented, not silently dropped.

modules/zero-crash/hooks/zero-fiction-gate.js (MC-SYS-74). PreToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit that scans tool_input.new_string|content|edits[].new_string for placeholder patterns. Two severity levels: HARD (Coming Soon, NotImplementedError, pass # TODO, Lorem ipsum, PLACEHOLDER token, throw new Error(not implemented)) returns permissionDecision:"ask" with reason; SOFT (TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments) returns additionalContext advisory. Bypassable via env ZERO_FICTION_GATE=off for legitimate placeholder writes. Verified 7/7 smoke: clean / soft-TODO / hard-Coming-Soon / hard-NotImplementedError / non-target Bash / env-disabled / MultiEdit hard. Wiring requires user adding hook to settings.json PreToolUse Edit|Write|MultiEdit matchers (BL-0009 settings.json session-load — activates next /restart).

BOM lesson documented in memory/feedback_python_utf8_bom.md (MC-SYS-75). PowerShell Out-File and many cross-tool writers add a UTF-8 BOM. Python read_text(encoding=utf-8) does NOT strip it; json.loads raises JSONDecodeError; the hook catches and returns None; the caller silently degrades. Fix: use encoding=utf-8-sig on the READ side for any file that may have been written externally. The WRITE side already uses os.O_BINARY in lib/atomic_write.py (sibling rule: feedback_windows_text_mode_compounding.md, BL-0014). Together these two rules form the complete cross-tool encoding contract.

MC-SYS-72 (Stop/PostToolUse hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext claimed Invalid input) REJECTED. User asserted the field does not exist in the base schema. Verified via claude-code-guide subagent against https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md: additionalContext IS supported for both Stop AND PostToolUse events. My implementation in context-watchdog.py is correct. No code change needed. Memory feedback_verify_schema_before_fix.md added so future schema-rejection claims trigger doc verification before any rewrite. This is the second SYS-XX rejection caught by empirical doc-check (sibling: BL-0023/0025 lazy-require + binary-format theatrical optimizations rejected for sub-ms savings).

zero-fiction-gate.js (BL-0038) wired into ~/.claude/settings.json PreToolUse via update-config skill (user explicitly authorized 2026-05-03). Entry appended at index 9 of PreToolUse array; matcher=Edit|Write|MultiEdit; timeout=5s. All 9 prior hooks preserved (indices 0-8: hook-dispatcher, secret-scanner, quality-gate, process-sandbox, gatekeeper-semantic, anti-thrash, ovo-push-gate, readonly-prompts-guard, skill-heat-map-advisor). JSON parses cleanly. Activates next /restart per BL-0009 (settings.json session-load rule). Verification: node -e parse confirmed entry shape + no regression in prior hooks.
